evidence_excerpt: Two years after diagnosis (age 6), the patient was clinically asymptomatic with full pain-free range of motion and no gait abnormalities, but radiographs still showed capital flattening and epiphyseal extrusion.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: At six years of age, she maintained a full pain-free range of motion without observed gait abnormalities.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: She was clinically asymptomatic at this point (two years after diagnosis).

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At age six (two years after diagnosis), she had a full pain-free range of motion and no gait abnormalities, whereas at diagnosis abduction was limited to 45°.
At age six, she was clinically asymptomatic.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time taken as the Legg-Calvé-Perthes diagnosis (~3 years 11 months) when nonoperative treatment was initiated. Follow-up extends to about two years post-diagnosis; long-term beyond that is not documented. Radiographic residual deformity persists despite clinical recovery.
